title: Conjecture 4
statement_text: For any sufficiently large odd integer $k$, $m(C_k) = (k-1)!/2$.
context_text: The extremal coloring $\chi(k, k-1)$ of $K_{2k-1}$ contains exactly $(k-1)!/2$ monochromatic copies of $C_k$ (all in the blue clique of order $k$), giving an upper bound on $m(C_k)$. The authors suggest this upper bound is in fact tight for all sufficiently large odd $k$, strengthening the $(ck)^k$ lower bound proved as Theorem 3.
paper_title: Threshold Ramsey multiplicity for odd cycles
